Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Secure Domain Name System (DNS) Deployment 2026 Guide [pdf] 75 upvotes, 7 comments
  2. Tony Hoare and His Imprint on Computer Science 42 upvotes, 4 comments
  3. Data Manipulation in Clojure Compared to R and Python 28 upvotes, 2 comments
  4. Wine 11 rewrites how Linux runs Windows games at kernel with massive speed gains 19 upvotes, 1 comments
  5. Debunking Zswap and Zram Myths 146 upvotes, 41 comments
  6. ARM AGI CPU: Specs and SKUs 54 upvotes, 12 comments
  7. Show HN: Gemini can now natively embed video, so I built sub-second video search 114 upvotes, 35 comments
  8. Ripgrep is faster than grep, ag, git grep, ucg, pt, sift (2016) 276 upvotes, 114 comments
  9. Show HN: Email.md – Markdown to responsive, email-safe HTML 68 upvotes, 22 comments
  10. The AI Industry Is Lying to You 78 upvotes, 27 comments
  11. No Terms. No Conditions 150 upvotes, 63 comments
  12. curl > /dev/sda: How I made a Linux distro that runs wget | dd 125 upvotes, 51 comments
  13. Country that put backdoors in Cisco routers to spy on world bans foreign routers 74 upvotes, 28 comments
  14. Hypura – A storage-tier-aware LLM inference scheduler for Apple Silicon 136 upvotes, 60 comments
  15. io_uring, libaio performance across Linux kernels and an unexpected IOMMU trap 46 upvotes, 16 comments
  16. Testing the Swift C compatibility with Raylib (+WASM) 41 upvotes, 15 comments
  17. Hypothesis, Antithesis, synthesis 115 upvotes, 56 comments
  18. How to use storytelling to fit inline assembly into Rust 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  19. Opera: Rewind The Web to 1996 (Opera at 30) 169 upvotes, 106 comments
  20. Microsoft's "Fix" for Windows 11: Flowers After the Beating 822 upvotes, 601 comments
  21. Nanobrew: The fastest macOS package manager compatible with brew 123 upvotes, 76 comments
  22. Arm AGI CPU 61 upvotes, 34 comments
  23. Atomic Display Switching: Solving 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  24. LaGuardia pilots raised safety alarms months before deadly runway crash 226 upvotes, 183 comments
  25. Apple Business 178 upvotes, 146 comments
  26. WolfGuard: WireGuard with FIPS 140-3 cryptography 51 upvotes, 36 comments
  27. So where are all the AI apps? 303 upvotes, 285 comments
  28. Show HN: ProofShot – Give AI coding agents eyes to verify the UI they build 75 upvotes, 60 comments
  29. Tell HN: Litellm 1.82.7 and 1.82.8 on PyPI are compromised 110 upvotes, 293 comments
  30. Lago (YC S21) Is Hiring 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 3:00am, 25 March 2026. Source here.
